Stages.summary: Isolate, activate, clarify, filter, and freeze PRP from anticoagulated human venous blood.
Synthesize GelMA from type A gelatin via methacrylation, purify by dialysis, and lyophilize.
Dissolve lyophilized GelMA in PBS with photoinitiator and sterile-filter to obtain the precursor solution.
Generate GelMA-PRP microdroplets by microfluidics and UV-cure them into hydrogel microspheres, then wash and collect.
Combine GelMA microspheres with PRP-containing hydrogel to form the final PRP@GelMA-M composite.

Materials: Phosphate buffered saline (PBS): ~110 mL total (20 mL + 80 mL + 10 mL)
Instruments: Microfluidic device (capillary glass tube, outlet 0.1 mm, channel 0.5 mm; Beijing Ucalery Technology)

Operations.Description: Combine 9 mL venous blood with 1 mL sodium citrate anticoagulant in a 15 mL centrifuge tube and centrifuge to separate blood into stratified layers.

Object: stratified blood — yellowish upper layer and junction layer
Operations.Description: Meticulously remove the yellowish portions of the upper layer and the junction between the upper and lower layers after centrifugation.
Centrifuge the collected fraction again to pellet platelets.

Object: serum (for dilution) or further centrifugation (for concentration)
Operations.Description: Adjust platelet concentration to 1 × 10^9 mL−1 by adding serum or further centrifugation. Then mix with 10% CaCl2 and thrombin in a 9:1 volume ratio (PRP mixture : activator).
Incubate the activated PRP mixture at 37 °C for 4 h to allow platelet activation and growth factor release.
Centrifuge the activated PRP to pellet debris and clarify the supernatant containing released growth factors.
Filter the clarified supernatant through a 0.22 μm filter to remove remaining particulates and sterilize.
Freeze the sterile PRP filtrate at −80 °C for storage until subsequent use.
Dissolve 2 g of type A gelatin in 20 mL of PBS at 60 °C.
Add 1.6 mL of methacrylic anhydride dropwise to the gelatin solution at a flow rate of 1.0 mL h−1 for 3 h under vigorous stirring to methacryloyl the gelatin.
Add 80 mL of prewarmed PBS to the reaction mixture, then dialyze using a dialysis tube with 12–14 kDa molecular weight cutoff for one week to remove unreacted components.
Filter the purified GelMA solution through a 0.22 μm filter.
Freeze the filtered GelMA solution at −80 °C overnight, then lyophilize (freeze-dry) to obtain dry GelMA.
Dissolve 0.7 g of lyophilized GelMA in 10 mL of PBS containing photoinitiator at 0.5% w/v concentration while maintaining the temperature at 60 °C.
Filter the GelMA precursor solution through a 0.22 μm filter to remove bacteria and obtain a sterile solution.

Object: sterile GelMA precursor solution with PRP (dispersed phase)
Operations.Container: microfluidic device (capillary glass tube outlet 0.1 mm, channel 0.5 mm; Beijing Ucalery Technology)
Operations.Description: Load GelMA-PRP mixture as the internal aqueous phase and paraffin oil with 5% Span 80 as the continuous phase into separate syringes. Inject into the microfluidic device at flow rates of 1.2 mL h−1 (dispersed phase) and 12 mL h−1 (continuous phase) using a syringe pump. Maintain production temperature at 20 °C. Shear force at the capillary outlet generates GelMA-PRP microdroplets.

Object: GelMA-PRP microdroplets in continuous oil phase
Operations.Description: Collect the microdroplets and cure them under UV irradiation to crosslink the GelMA and form hydrogel microspheres.

UV irradiation: not specified — wavelength and intensity not reported

Operations.Description: Wash the microspheres with double-distilled water to remove oil and surfactant.
Centrifuge the microsphere suspension several times to pellet the microspheres and remove residual wash liquid.

centrifuge: not reported — speed and duration not specified

Operations.Description: Add the pelleted GelMA microspheres to hydrogels that have been mixed with different concentrations of PRP to obtain the final PRP@GelMA hydrogel microspheres composite (PRP@GelMA-M).

Missing information: UV irradiation wavelength and intensity for microsphere curing (not reported)
Centrifuge speed and duration for microsphere washing steps (not reported)
Exact PRP concentrations used in the composite hydrogel (described as 'different concentrations' without specification)
Container types for several intermediate steps
Whether UV curing occurs on-chip or post-collection in the microfluidic device
